name: Electrical Substation IED Manipulation
description: Compromise Intelligent Electronic Devices (IEDs) in an electrical substation to manipulate protective relays, circuit breakers, and bus configurations via IEC 61850 / DNP3 protocols, causing cascading outages.
root_objective: Manipulate substation IEDs to cause unplanned load shedding or cascading grid faults

nodes.title: Manipulate Substation IEDs for Grid Disruption
nodes.description: Gain control of substation Intelligent Electronic Devices to open breakers, disable protective relays, or mis-operate tap changers, causing localised or cascading outages.

nodes.title: Compromise via Corporate IT to OT Pivot
nodes.description: Gain initial access through the utility's corporate network and pivot across the IT/OT boundary into the substation LAN.

nodes.title: Direct Protocol-Level Attack on IEC 61850
nodes.description: Access the substation process bus directly and inject GOOSE or MMS messages to mis-operate IEDs without authenticating.

nodes.title: Phish Utility SCADA Engineer
nodes.description: Spearphish an engineer with access to SCADA/EMS systems, delivering a RAT that provides initial corporate network access.

nodes.title: Traverse IT/OT Firewall via Jump Host
nodes.description: Compromise the engineering jump host / data historian that straddles the IT/OT DMZ to reach the substation IED network.

nodes.title: Abuse DNP3 Outstation Commands
nodes.description: Once on the OT LAN, send crafted DNP3 commands to trip breakers or change relay setpoints. Many legacy RTUs lack authentication.

nodes.title: Gain Physical Access to Substation Cabinet
nodes.description: Bypass perimeter fencing and locks to reach relay cabinets and connect directly to the IEC 61850 process bus via engineering ports.

nodes.title: Inject Spoofed GOOSE Messages
nodes.description: Broadcast forged GOOSE (Generic Object-Oriented Substation Event) frames on the process bus to trigger breaker operations. GOOSE lacks native authentication in most deployments.

nodes.title: GOOSE Messages Lack Authentication
nodes.description: IEC 62351 authentication for GOOSE is rarely implemented; most substations accept any publisher on the process bus VLAN.

nodes.title: Modify Relay Protection Settings via MMS
nodes.description: Use Manufacturing Message Specification (MMS) to write new protection curve parameters, causing relays to mis-trip or fail to trip under fault conditions.

nodes.title: Knowledge of Substation Layout & Vendor Equipment
nodes.description: Attacker must obtain drawings, single-line diagrams, or vendor documentation to know which IEDs control which feeders.

nodes.title: Data Historian Straddles IT/OT Boundary
nodes.description: The PI/OSIsoft historian server has interfaces on both the corporate and OT networks, providing a natural pivot point into substation systems.
